act1.subject: VPN Upgrade - Instructions for Remote Access
act1.body:
Hi all,

We're rolling out a new VPN client starting this week. Please uninstall the old GlobalProtect version and download the new one from the IT portal by end of day Friday.

Instructions:
1. Go to intranet.veridian-corp.com/it/vpn
2. Download the installer for your OS
3. Run the installer and log in with your network credentials
4. Contact helpdesk@veridian-corp.com if you run into issues

Remote access will be unavailable during the migration window (Saturday Jun 27, 8 PM to Sunday Jun 28, 6 AM).

Thanks,
IT Support

act1.subject: New Expense Policy - Effective July 5
act1.body:
Dear colleagues,

Starting July 5, the updated expense reimbursement policy goes into effect. Key changes:

- Meal receipts required for any amount over $25 (previously $50)
- Travel pre-approval now required 5 business days in advance
- New expense categories added for client entertainment

Full policy document is available on the HR portal. Please review before submitting your next expense report.

Questions? Contact hr-support@veridian-corp.com.

Best regards,
HR Department

act1.subject: Conference Room Booking - 2:30 PM Jun 23
act1.body:
Daniel,

Booked Maple Room for tomorrow at 2:30. Just need 30 minutes to go over the Henderson deliverables before the client call Wednesday.

Bring the latest scope doc if you have it. I want to double-check the timeline numbers before we present.

Let me know if you need to move it.

James

act1.subject: Ansible Group - Project Lead Selection
act1.body:
Daniel, Paul,

I've been in discussions with the board about who should lead the Ansible Group pursuit. This is going to be one of the biggest contracts we've gone after this year, and I want the right person driving it.

Both of you have the client relationship skills and the track record. I'd like to hear from each of you: send me a quick note on how you'd approach the first 60 days if you were leading this.

I'll make the call by end of week.

Richard

act1.subject: Re: Ansible Group - Project Lead Selection
act1.body:
Richard,

Appreciate you thinking of me. Honestly, the Ansible thing looks like an exciting opportunity, but I've got a lot on my plate right now with the Meridian account and the Q3 planning cycle. I'd rather not stretch myself too thin and end up doing a mediocre job on something this important.

Daniel would be great for this. He's got the bandwidth and the relationship with their VP of procurement is already solid from the Henderson work last year.

Happy to support however I can from the sidelines.

Paul

act1.subject: Re: Ansible Group - Project Lead Selection
act1.body:
Daniel,

You're leading the Ansible pursuit. I want a preliminary strategy deck on my desk by Friday. We're meeting with their team in two weeks and I want us sharp.

Paul, thanks for the candor. I'll loop you in if we need support on the technical side.

This is a big one. Don't let me down.

Richard

act1.subject: Thursday?
act1.body:
Hey,

Are you free Thursday evening? I was thinking we could try that place on 4th street. The one with the rooftop.

Let me know.

S.

act1.subject: Greenside Thursday?
act1.body:
Dan!

Courts are booked for Thursday at 6:30: me, Tom from Arcadia, and a guy from the second floor whose name I keep forgetting. We need a fourth. You in?

Greenside Padel Club, same as usual. They just resurfaced court 2, it's beautiful.

P.

act1.subject: Re: Thursday
act1.body:
Perfect. I'll make a reservation for 7:30. That gives you time if you're doing that padel thing first.

Can't wait.

S.

act1.subject: Ansible - Timeline and Expectations
act1.body:
Daniel,

Now that you're leading this, here's what I need from you. Timeline for the Ansible pursuit:

- Jul 3: Internal strategy review (you, me, legal)
- Jul 7: First client meeting (their offices, bring the preliminary deck)
- Jul 14: Revised proposal due
- Jul 17: Final presentation and contract signing (tentative)

I expect weekly updates starting Friday. Don't wait for me to ask.

The board is watching this one. We need it.

Richard

act1.subject: Lunch plans - Friday 12:30?
act1.body:
Hey Daniel,

A few of us are going to Nori for lunch Friday. 12:30. You in?

Lisa

act1.subject: Re: Greenside Thursday
act1.body:
Great game last night! You almost had me on that last set. Almost.

Rematch next week? Same time, same place. I'll book the court.

Also, Tom says he's bringing his brother next time. Guy apparently played semi-pro in Spain. We're going to get destroyed.

P.

act1.subject: Phoenix Project - Weekly Status Update
act1.body:
Team,

Phoenix Project status for the week of Jun 22:

Completed:
- User authentication module: passed QA, deployed to staging
- Database migration scripts for v2.3: ready for review
- Client feedback survey: sent to 200 accounts, 45 responses so far

In Progress:
- API integration with third-party vendor (Daniel, need your sign-off on the contract terms)
- Dashboard redesign: wireframes under review
- Performance testing: scheduled for next Tuesday

Blockers:
- Waiting on legal for the vendor contract language (Daniel, can you nudge them?)

Next week's priorities:
- Finalize vendor contract
- Complete dashboard wireframes
- Begin load testing

David

act1.subject: Mid-Year Performance Review Cycle - Self-Assessment Due Jul 4
act1.body:
Dear Daniel Hartman,

The mid-year performance review cycle has begun. Please complete your self-assessment by July 4.

Access the form at: hr-portal.veridian-corp.com/reviews

Your manager will submit their feedback after your self-assessment is received. The review meeting will be scheduled within two weeks of submission.

Thank you,
HR Department

act1.subject: Quick question
act1.body:
Hey,

Did Richard say anything about the client dinner next week? He mentioned it to me in passing but I didn't catch the details. I need to book the restaurant if it's happening.

Also, I accidentally CC'd my personal email on this, ignore that. Was sending something to myself earlier and forgot to remove it.

Sarah

act1.subject: Where are we on this?
act1.body:
Daniel,

I haven't seen the preliminary strategy deck. Friday means Friday.

Where are we?

Richard

act1.subject: Can you review the Henderson proposal?
act1.body:
Daniel,

Can you take a look at the Henderson proposal before it goes out? I made the revisions we talked about in the meeting Tuesday but want a second pair of eyes on the pricing section.

Link: docs.veridian-corp.com/henderson-q1-proposal-v3

No rush, Monday is fine.

David

act1.subject: Parking Garage Closure - Next Week
act1.body:
Hi all,

The B2 level of the parking garage will be closed for maintenance from Monday Jun 29 through Wednesday Jul 1. Please use B1 or the surface lot during this time.

Apologies for the inconvenience.

Karen Walsh
Facilities Management

act1.subject: Weekend doubles - Saturday?
act1.body:
Dan,

Few of us are playing doubles tomorrow afternoon at Greenside. 3 PM, courts 1 and 2. Casual, no pressure. Grab a beer after.

You in?

P.

act1.subject: Re: Quick question
act1.body:
Thank you for last night. I had a really good time.

The restaurant was perfect. We should go back.

S.

act1.subject: Ansible - Status Update and Insights
act1.body:
Daniel,

I want to give you an edge on this Ansible pitch. I've been talking to a contact of mine at Ansible Group, Greg Nolan. He's been there for years, knows how their procurement team thinks.

Greg shared some internal feedback from their last vendor review. Looks like they're unhappy with their current provider on response times and contract flexibility. That's our angle.

DO NOT share this outside our team. Greg is doing us a favor and I don't want to burn the bridge.

Use this in your strategy deck. You'll need it for the Jul 3 review.

Richard

act1.subject: Lunch?
act1.body:
Free for lunch today? I have something I want to talk to you about. Nothing bad, promise.

The usual place?

S.

act1.subject: Building Security Training - July 6
act1.body:
Hi Daniel,

Reminder that the annual building security training is scheduled for July 6 at 10 AM in the main conference room. Attendance is mandatory per corporate policy.

If you have a conflict, please let me know by Friday so I can schedule you for the makeup session on July 10.

Thanks,
Rachel

act1.subject: FW: Emily update
act1.body:
Forwarding this because it made me smile. Emily's doing well. She's been a good friend to me since I started here. We used to grab lunch together every week when she was still on the fourth floor.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Emily Tan
To: Sarah Colvin
Date: Mon Jun 29

Hey S!

Just wanted to say hi. Things are good over here. Work is the same old, but the padel group has been keeping me sane. Good session Saturday. I'm still terrible but at least I'm consistently terrible.

We should grab coffee this week. Thursday?

Em

act1.subject: Ansible - Need Competitive Analysis ASAP
act1.body:
Daniel,

I need a full competitive analysis on the Ansible deal by Thursday. Who else is bidding, what they're offering, where we beat them.

Talk to Lisa, she ran something similar for the Protex account last year. Don't reinvent the wheel.

This is priority one. Push back everything else if you have to.

Richard

act1.subject: Meeting Request - Ansible Prep
act1.body:
Daniel,

Can we get 45 minutes before the Jul 3 internal strategy review? I want to make sure the deliverables section is tight before Richard sees it.

I'm open Wednesday afternoon or Thursday morning.

James

act1.subject: Company All-Hands Recap - Q2 Results
act1.body:
Dear Veridian team,

Thank you to everyone who joined yesterday's all-hands. For those who couldn't attend, here's a summary:

Q2 highlights:
- Revenue up 12% year-over-year
- 23 new enterprise accounts signed
- Employee satisfaction score: 4.2/5 (up from 3.9)
- New Portland office opens in October

Priorities for the second half:
- Expand enterprise sales (pipeline target: $45M)
- Launch v3.0 of the platform by year end
- Grow headcount by 15% across engineering and sales

Recording available on the intranet. Slides attached.

Onward,
The CEO Office

act1.subject: Competitor Activity - FYI
act1.body:
Daniel,

Heard through the grapevine that Stratton & Marsh are also going after the Ansible contract. They've been aggressive: undercutting on price and promising faster implementation timelines.

My contact at Ansible says their team is split. Some prefer us, some prefer S&M's pricing.

We need to sharpen our value prop. Price isn't where we win this. It's trust and track record. Make sure the deck reflects that. The strategy review is Friday and I want this addressed.

Don't ask me where I got this. Just use it.

Richard

act1.subject: Tonight?
act1.body:
Are you around tonight? I could come over after 8.

S.

act1.subject: Court booking confirmed - Thursday 6:30
act1.body:
Court 2 locked in for Thursday at 6:30. Just us and Tom this week, his brother bailed. Something about a flight delay from Barcelona.

Bring your A game. Last week was embarrassing for you.

P.

act1.subject: Need you
act1.body:
Richard is in your office. He's looking for you and he's not in a patient mood. Something about the deck.

Come save me.

S.

act1.subject: Ansible - Confidential FYI
act1.body:
Daniel,

Between us: I've been in contact with someone on Ansible's evaluation committee. They're leaning our way but there's one board member who's a holdout. Apparently he has a personal relationship with someone at Stratton & Marsh.

I'm working on it. Don't put this in any document. If anyone asks, we're winning this on merit alone.

Keep your head down and deliver the deck. The Jul 7 client meeting is a week away.

Richard

act1.subject: Training Module - Compliance Required
act1.body:
Dear Daniel Hartman,

Our records show you have not yet completed this quarter's mandatory compliance training module. Please complete it by July 11 to remain in compliance with company policy.

Access the module at: hr-portal.veridian-corp.com/training

Estimated time: 45 minutes.

Thank you,
HR Department

act1.subject: Client Visit Next Week - Reminder
act1.body:
Hi team,

Quick reminder that the Protex team is visiting our office next Wednesday (Jul 8) for their quarterly review. They'll be here from 10 AM to 3 PM.

Please make sure the 4th floor conference room is presentable and that the presentation materials are printed by Tuesday EOD.

Daniel, can you stop by for the first 15 minutes to say hello? They always ask about you.

Thanks,
Lisa

act1.subject: Weekend plans?
act1.body:
Hey,

Any chance you're free this weekend? Saturday afternoon? I don't want to be alone.

S.

act1.subject: Team Lunch Next Thursday - RSVPs?
act1.body:
Hi everyone,

I'm organizing a team lunch for next Thursday at noon. Thinking about that Greek place on Main Street, Elara's.

Please let me know if you're in so I can make a reservation. Also flag any dietary restrictions I should know about.

Rachel

act1.subject: Re: Phoenix Project - Weekly Status Update
act1.body:
Team,

Quick Phoenix update for the week:

- Vendor contract language came back from legal with revisions. Daniel, I need you to review and approve by Monday. They want final signatures by Wednesday.
- Dashboard wireframes approved by the client. Moving to high-fidelity mockups next week.
- Load testing pushed to Thursday due to staging environment issues.

New action item for Daniel: the vendor contract is blocking the API integration. This has been sitting for a week now. Priority please.

David

act1.subject: Your review - let's meet Monday
act1.body:
Daniel,

Your self-assessment came through. Let's meet Monday at 3 PM in my office to go over your review.

Nothing to worry about. Routine.

Richard

act1.subject: FW: Emily's new thing
act1.body:
Found this going through old emails: Emily a few months back, right when she got into her padel group. She was SO happy. She's seemed off these past few days and I keep coming back to this. Probably nothing.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Emily Tan
To: Sarah Colvin
Date: Fri Feb 20, 2026

S!!

Ok so the padel group I told you about? It's turning into a whole thing. We went out for drinks after the Saturday session and I met some really interesting people. Not just office-park people, people from all over. One guy works in government consulting, another one runs a tech startup.

I feel like I finally belong somewhere, you know? Like these people actually see me. Not just somebody's assistant but actually ME.

You should come play sometime! I'll ask if I can bring a guest.

Em

act1.subject: Re: Weekend plans?
act1.body:
Yesterday was perfect. Thank you for making time. I know you've been buried with the Ansible stuff and the review on Monday.

I needed that more than you know.

S.

act1.subject: Ansible Deal - Status Call Required
act1.body:
Daniel,

Status call. Today. 4 PM. My office.

The board is asking questions and I need answers. The client meeting is tomorrow. Where are we on the revised proposal? Did you talk to Lisa about the competitive analysis?

Don't be late.

Richard

act1.subject: FW: Client Feedback (Confidential)
act1.body:
Daniel,

More from Greg. Their internal review committee met Friday. Our proposal is currently ranked #1 but Stratton & Marsh are close behind. The holdout board member I mentioned last week is pushing hard for S&M.

Greg says we need to add something to the implementation section: they want a dedicated account manager for the first 6 months. That wasn't in our original scope but it could seal it.

Make it happen. Add it to the revised proposal before the Jul 14 deadline. I'll handle the cost implications with the board.

DO NOT forward this to anyone.

Richard

act1.subject: Greenside admin - quarterly report update
act1.body:
Hey Dan,

Quick Greenside thing: we're putting together the club's quarterly stats for the membership committee. You know how it is with these places, they want numbers to justify the court bookings.

Can you bring the quarterly report when you come Saturday? Just the summary page is fine. I need to cross-reference some of our session attendance numbers.

Also Saturday is looking like a big group, 8 people confirmed so far. Should be a good time.

P.

act1.subject: Checking in
act1.body:
Hey,

Just thinking about you. Hope your day isn't too crazy with the Ansible prep.

I put the updated Ansible files on your desk this morning. Richard's been asking for the revised numbers so I pulled them before he could yell at you about it.

Talk later?

S.

act1.subject: FW: Emily - I need to get out
act1.body:
Daniel, I don't know what to make of this. Emily sent me this last night and I'm worried. Remember those emails of hers I forwarded you? She was fine, and now this. She's not usually like this. Should I be concerned?

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Emily Tan
To: Sarah Colvin
Date: Mon Jul 6

Sarah,

I can't keep doing this. I thought it was something good but it's not. The people I told you about, they're not what I thought. I need to get out but I don't know how. I feel like I'm in over my head.

I know this sounds dramatic. I'm probably overreacting. But something doesn't feel right and I don't know who to talk to.

Can we meet this week? In person, not at the office.

Em

act1.subject: Can you sign off on the Henderson scope?
act1.body:
Daniel,

The Henderson project scope document needs your sign-off before I can send it to procurement. I've attached the final version with the changes from last week's review.

Can you look at it today? They've been asking about this since the client call two weeks ago and they're getting antsy.

Tom

act1.subject: Ansible Prep Meeting - Moved to Thursday
act1.body:
Daniel,

Moving our Ansible prep to Thursday at 10 AM instead of Wednesday. I've got a conflict with the Protex visit, Lisa needs me there for the quarterly review.

Same agenda: deck review, deliverables check, dry run of the opening pitch.

Let me know if Thursday doesn't work.

James

act1.subject: System Maintenance Window - July 8 Night
act1.body:
Hi all,

Scheduled maintenance tomorrow night (Wednesday July 8) from 10 PM to 4 AM. The following systems will be unavailable:

- Email (webmail and Outlook)
- VPN
- Internal file sharing
- HR portal

Please save any work and plan accordingly. Systems will be restored by 4 AM Thursday.

IT Support

act1.subject: if you need to reach me after hours
act1.body:
Hey,

With the system going down tonight I realized you don't have a way to reach me after hours if work email is out.

If you ever need me, use my personal email. The password is [RANDOMIZED]. You'll know why. And yes, I'm giving you my password. I trust you. Don't judge me.

Use it if work email is down. Or just whenever.

S.

act1.subject: Ansible - WHERE ARE WE
act1.body:
Daniel,

I asked for the revised proposal MONDAY. It's Wednesday.

WHERE ARE WE.

The client meeting is next Tuesday. Greg tells me Stratton & Marsh submitted their revised bid yesterday. We are running out of time. I will not walk in there unprepared.

Get this to me by end of day or we're having a very different conversation.

Richard

act1.subject: Busy this weekend?
act1.body:
Dan,

Any interest in Saturday afternoon? I know you've been slammed with Ansible but you could use a break. Nothing like smashing a ball to relieve stress.

2 PM, Greenside. Big group again, same crew as last week plus a few new faces.

Let me know.

P.

act1.subject: I'm scared
act1.body:
Daniel,

I don't know how to say this. Something is wrong. I can feel it. I've been trying to ignore it but I can't anymore.

That thing with Emily, it's not just work stress. I think something is really wrong. I can't explain over email. Can we talk tonight? In person. Please.

I'm scared.

S.

act1.subject: Expense Reports Due Friday
act1.body:
Hi Daniel,

Friendly reminder that Q2 expense reports are due this Friday, July 10. Please submit through the finance portal by 5 PM.

If you have any outstanding receipts, please attach them to the submission. We can't process reimbursements without documentation.

Thanks,
Karen Walsh
Finance Department

act1.subject: Saw Sarah at Greenside last Saturday
act1.body:
Dan,

Random: I saw Sarah at Greenside last Saturday. Didn't know she played! She was with a group on court 3 while we were on 1. She's getting surprisingly good.

Small world. You should both come next time, we'll do mixed doubles.

P.

act1.subject: Mid-Year All-Hands Agenda - Input Needed
act1.body:
Hey team,

I'm putting together the agenda for the mid-year all-hands next week. If you have anything you want included (project updates, team wins, shout-outs), send it to me by Wednesday.

Daniel, can you do a 5-minute update on the Ansible deal? Richard mentioned it's close and the company should hear about it.

David

act1.subject: Client Expectations (Confidential)
act1.body:
Daniel,

Latest from Greg: Ansible's CFO is pushing for a 15% discount on the first year. Their procurement team is using Stratton & Marsh's pricing as leverage.

I told Greg we can do 10% with a 3-year commitment. He thinks that'll fly but wants us to frame it as an "early partnership discount" rather than a straight price cut.

Put it in the proposal under a new section. Make it look strategic, not desperate. The Jul 14 deadline is Tuesday.

And Daniel, I know I don't need to say this, but Greg's involvement stays between us. If anyone at Ansible finds out he's been feeding us intel, we lose the deal and he loses his job.

Richard

act1.subject: Re: Ansible Prep Meeting - Moved to Thursday
act1.body:
Daniel,

Good session yesterday. The deck is in solid shape. A few notes:

- Slide 14: the ROI projections need sourcing. Can you pull the Gartner numbers?
- Slide 22: Richard wants the implementation timeline compressed by 2 weeks. I think that's aggressive but it's his call.
- Opening pitch: I'd lead with the case study, not the company overview. Hook them early.

I'll send my edits by Monday morning. We should be ready for the Jul 14 submission.

James

act1.subject: Does Sarah have the Q2 files?
act1.body:
Hey Daniel,

I need the Q2 client satisfaction files for the Protex review. The visit yesterday went fine but they asked for the data and I promised I'd send it today.

I emailed Sarah twice but haven't heard back. Is she out today?

Can you check if they're on her desk or in the shared drive?

Thanks,
Lisa

act1.subject: Phoenix Project - Sprint Recap and Next Steps
act1.body:
Team,

Sprint recap for the week of Jul 6:

Completed:
- Vendor contract signed (thanks Daniel for finally pushing legal on this)
- High-fidelity mockups delivered for dashboard redesign
- Load testing completed: performance within acceptable thresholds

In Progress:
- API integration: development started, estimated completion Jul 18
- Client UAT preparation: test scripts being written
- Bug fixes from QA round 2 (12 issues, 8 resolved, 4 remaining)

Next sprint priorities:
- Complete API integration
- Begin client UAT
- Resolve remaining QA bugs

We're on track for the July 27 milestone. Good work everyone.

David

act1.subject: Benefits Enrollment Reminder - Deadline July 19
act1.body:
Dear colleagues,

This is a reminder that the annual benefits enrollment window closes on July 19. If you wish to make any changes to your health, dental, vision, or retirement plan selections, please do so before the deadline.

Access the benefits portal at: hr-portal.veridian-corp.com/benefits

If you do not make any changes, your current selections will automatically roll over.

Questions? Contact benefits@veridian-corp.com.

HR Department

act1.subject: Can you handle the Q2 project directly?
act1.body:
Daniel,

I've been trying to get the Q2 project files from Sarah for the Henderson close-out but she seems to be out. I've emailed her three times since Thursday and nothing. Nobody on the floor has heard from her since last week.

Is she on PTO? I don't see anything on the calendar.

In the meantime, can you pull the files directly? I'm running out of time on this: procurement needs the final package by Wednesday.

James

act1.subject: FYI - New org chart is up on the intranet
act1.body:
Daniel,

New org chart is posted on the intranet. A few moves in the sales org. Looks like they're consolidating the West Coast teams under one VP.

Nothing changes for us but figured you'd want to see it.

Lisa

act1.subject: Did you get my voicemail?
act1.body:
Daniel,

Left you a voicemail about the team lunch Thursday, Rachel's thing at Elara's. Did you get it? I need a headcount by tomorrow to confirm the reservation.

Also, are you ok? You seemed distracted in the standup this morning.

Rachel

act1.subject: Is Sarah on PTO?
act1.body:
Daniel,

Sorry to bother you again about this. I still haven't heard from Sarah. It's been almost a week now. I checked the PTO calendar and she's not listed as being out.

Is everything ok? It's not like her to go dark for this long.

Lisa

act1.subject: Sarah's account - Can you access her files?
act1.body:
Daniel,

I need the vendor comparison spreadsheet that Sarah was maintaining for the Henderson project. She hasn't responded to any of my emails or calls since last Thursday.

Do you have access to her files? Or can you check with IT to get temporary access to her shared drive? I'm stuck without this document and James is on me about the close-out.

Thanks,
Tom

act1.subject: How was your weekend?
act1.body:
Dan,

How was your weekend? Missed you at Greenside Saturday, we had a full house. Tom's brother finally came back and destroyed us all. The guy's forehand is from another planet. My ego is still recovering.

Hope everything's good with you. You've been quiet lately.

P.

act1.subject: Ansible - FINAL Push
act1.body:
Daniel,

The final presentation is Friday. Three days. I need:

1. Updated proposal with the partnership discount section Greg suggested
2. Final deck with all of James's edits incorporated
3. Printed copies: 6 sets, bound

I don't care what else is on your plate. This is it. Everything else can wait.

If we lose this deal because of preparation, it's on you.

Richard

act1.subject: Elevator Maintenance - July 15 to 17
act1.body:
Dear colleagues,

The east wing elevators will be out of service for maintenance from Wednesday July 15 through Friday July 17. Please use the west wing elevators or the stairwells during this period.

We apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your patience.

Facilities Management

act1.subject: Sarah Colvin - no building access logged since July 9
act1.body:
Daniel,

I'm reaching out because our building access system shows that Sarah Colvin's badge hasn't scanned at any entry point since Thursday, July 9.

Her badge was last used at 8:47 AM on July 9 at the main entrance. Nothing since then: no turnstile scans, no parking garage entry, no after-hours access.

Is she working from home? I don't have a remote work request on file for her. Just want to make sure everything's in order from a security standpoint.

Please let me know when you get a chance.

Karen Walsh
HR / Facilities

act1.subject: Ansible - Contract Signature Pending
act1.body:
Daniel,

Ansible's legal team sent back the contract with minor redlines. Nothing substantive, just language adjustments in the liability section. Our legal is reviewing now. Signature target is still Friday.

This is happening. Don't lose focus.

Richard

act1.subject: Phoenix standup notes - July 15
act1.body:
Daniel,

Standup notes from this morning:

- API integration: on track, 70% complete
- Client UAT: test scripts ready, scheduling with the client this week
- QA bugs: 2 remaining, both minor UI issues
- Dashboard: final review with the client Thursday

Action items for you:
- Review the UAT test plan (attached, 3 pages)
- Confirm the July 27 demo date with the client

Let me know if you need anything.

David

act1.subject: Office Birthday Party - Friday 3 PM
act1.body:
Hi everyone,

We're celebrating July birthdays this Friday at 3 PM in the break room. Cake and coffee provided.

This month's honorees: James Porter, Karen Walsh, and David Kim.

Come say happy birthday and take a break!

Rachel

act1.subject: Vendor Invoice - Need Your Approval
act1.body:
Daniel,

Got a vendor invoice that needs your approval before I can process it. It's for the API integration tools we ordered for Phoenix: $4,200, matching the PO we submitted in February.

Can you approve it in the finance portal today? The vendor's payment terms are net 15 and we're already at day 10.

Link: finance.veridian-corp.com/approvals/pending

Tom

act1.subject: I'm really concerned about Sarah
act1.body:
Daniel,

I don't want to overstep but I'm really worried about Sarah. Nobody has heard from her in a week. She's not answering her phone, not responding to emails, and she's not on the PTO calendar.

I asked around and nobody knows anything. James said you might know what's going on since you work with her the most closely.

Tom has been asking about the Henderson files too and can't get through to her.

Is she ok? Should we be doing something?

Lisa

act1.subject: Performance Review - Your Manager's Feedback is Ready
act1.body:
Dear Daniel Hartman,

Your manager has submitted their feedback for the mid-year performance review cycle. You can now view the complete review, including your self-assessment and your manager's comments.

Access your review at: hr-portal.veridian-corp.com/reviews/my-review

Please schedule a follow-up meeting with your manager within the next 10 business days to discuss the results.

Thank you,
HR Department

act1.subject: Want to play Friday?
act1.body:
Dan,

Courts are available Friday at 5:30. You, me, Tom. Quick session before the weekend.

You've been MIA lately. Everything alright?

P.

act1.subject: Ansible Deal - CLOSED
act1.body:
Daniel,

They signed. Ansible Group is ours.

$2.4 million, 3-year contract. Biggest deal we've closed this quarter. The board is thrilled. I told them it was a team effort but between us, you delivered.

Greg sends his congratulations too. We'll announce at the all-hands next week. Drinks tonight, on me.

Good work.

Richard

act1.subject: Can anyone reach Sarah?
act1.body:
Daniel,

I hate to bring this up today of all days with the Ansible news, but I really need those Henderson files and I genuinely cannot reach Sarah. I've called her personal phone, emailed her work and personal addresses, even stopped by her desk again this morning.

Her computer is still there. Her jacket is still on her chair. It's like she just didn't come back one day.

Do you know anything? I'm starting to get a bad feeling about this.

James

act1.subject: We need to call the police
act1.body:
Daniel,

I've just been briefed on the Sarah situation. Karen from facilities flagged it and James Porter came to me directly. I should have been told sooner.

No one has heard from her in over a week. Her badge hasn't been used since July 9. Her phone is going straight to voicemail. This isn't PTO and this isn't a personal day.

I'm calling the police this afternoon. If you know anything, anything at all, now is the time to tell me.

And Daniel, given your working relationship with her, they're going to want to talk to you first.

Richard

act1.subject: See you Friday night?
act1.body:
Dan,

Congrats on the Ansible deal! Heard the good news. You must be relieved after all those weeks of Richard breathing down your neck.

A few of us are playing at Greenside tonight around 7 if you want to celebrate properly. Nothing like a win and a good match to end the week.

Let me know. Court's booked either way.

P.

act1.subject: Weekly digest: here's what you missed
act1.body:
Your weekly digest from Microsoft Teams

3 unread messages in General
2 mentions in Phoenix Project
1 new file shared in Ansible Group

View in Teams ->

act1.subject: 3 new replies in Phoenix Project
act1.body:
Phoenix Project channel

David Kim replied to "API vendor selection":
"I've narrowed it down to two options. Can we discuss Thursday?"

2 other replies

View in Teams ->

act1.body:

act1.subject: Weekly digest: here's what you missed
act1.body:
Your weekly digest from Microsoft Teams

2 unread messages in General
1 mention in Phoenix Project
1 new file shared in General

View in Teams ->

act1.subject: Your week - schedule notes
act1.body:
Good morning,

Quick notes on your week:

- Tuesday 9 AM moved to 10 AM (Richard's request, he has a board call)
- Wednesday: Protex QBR prep with Lisa, 2 PM, Maple Room
- Thursday: I blocked 2 hours for the Henderson close-out review
- Your dry cleaning receipt is with the expense pile on your desk

The Q2 satisfaction files you asked about are in the shared drive under /clients/protex/2026.

Sarah

act1.subject: Security Awareness - Phishing Module Due
act1.body:
Hi all,

This quarter's security awareness module is now live: "Recognizing Phishing and Social Engineering."

All staff must complete it by July 3. Estimated time: 20 minutes.

Remember: never share your password with anyone, and report suspicious emails to security@veridian-corp.com.

IT Support

act1.subject: HVAC Maintenance - 4th Floor - Wednesday
act1.body:
Dear colleagues,

HVAC maintenance is scheduled for the 4th floor this Wednesday between 7 AM and noon. Expect some noise and temperature variation during the morning.

Meeting rooms on 4 remain bookable but we recommend moving sensitive calls to floor 3.

Facilities Management

act1.subject: Phoenix Project - Weekly Status Update
act1.body:
Team,

Phoenix status for the week of Jun 15:

Completed:
- QA round 1 closed out: 14 issues logged, 9 resolved
- Staging environment rebuilt after last week's outage

In Progress:
- User authentication module: in QA now
- Database migration scripts for v2.3
- Client feedback survey draft (going out next week)

Blockers:
- None this week.

David

act1.subject: Fire Warden Volunteers Needed
act1.body:
Hi everyone,

We need two volunteer fire wardens for the 5th floor following January's reorganization. Training is a half day, provided by the building.

If you're interested, reply to me by Friday. If nobody volunteers, I will volunteer someone. You know I will.

Rachel

act1.subject: Court Thursday 6:30?
act1.body:
Dan,

Usual court, usual time tonight? Tom's in. His backhand is still terrible, so we're keeping him.

Greenside, 6:30. Loser buys.

P.

act1.subject: Protex QBR - Prep Doc
act1.body:
Daniel,

Draft prep doc for the Protex quarterly review is in the shared drive. I flagged two renewal risks in section 3, take a look before our session next week.

Nothing alarming, just want us aligned before they visit next month.

Lisa

act1.subject: February Payslips Available
act1.body:
Dear colleagues,

February payslips are now available on the HR portal under My Documents > Payroll.

As a reminder, the portal password reset process changed in January. If you're locked out, contact hr-support@veridian-corp.com.

HR Department

act1.subject: Henderson close-out - timeline check
act1.body:
Daniel,

Before things get busy: are we still targeting mid-August for the Henderson close-out? Procurement asked and I said I'd confirm.

If the timeline's moved, let me know so I can manage expectations on their side.

Have a good weekend,
James

act2.subject: You should join us
act2.body:
Sarah,

I know this is a bit random but I got your personal email from the group chat at the office happy hour last month. Hope that's ok.

A bunch of us play padel at the Greenside Padel Club near the office on Saturday afternoons. It's casual, no experience needed. Good people, good exercise, and the courts are right off the highway so it's easy to get to.

You should come out sometime. This Saturday we're starting at 3 PM. No commitment, just show up and see if you like it.

Paul

act2.subject: I'm so glad you came Saturday
act2.body:
Sarah!!

That was so much fun! I'm so glad you came. The people are so nice, right? I've been going for a few weeks now and it already feels like my favorite part of the week.

I know it sounds cheesy but I actually feel like I belong with this group. Like they actually care about us as people, not just as "the assistants." Nobody talks to me like that at work.

We should go together next Saturday. I'll pick you up.

Em

act2.subject: Saturday match - 3 PM courts 1 and 2
act2.body:
Hi all,

Courts 1 and 2 booked for this Saturday, 3 PM. Looks like we'll have 10 people so we can run two full matches.

Bring water and a change of clothes. We'll probably grab food after at the usual place.

If you're bringing a guest, reply to this email so we can plan accordingly.

See you there.

act2.subject: Re: You should join us
act2.body:
Paul,

I wanted to say thanks for inviting me. I've been to three sessions now and I really enjoy it. Everyone's been so welcoming. I wasn't sure at first because I'm terrible at anything athletic but nobody made me feel bad about it.

It's nice to have something outside of work that feels like mine.

See you Saturday.

Sarah

act2.subject: You have to meet some people from the group
act2.body:
Sarah,

There are some people in the group you really need to meet. Not the padel regulars. The inner circle, I guess? Paul introduced me to a few of them last week. They're doing really interesting things outside of work. One guy consults for government agencies, another one runs an investment group.

They care about making a real difference. Not like the people at the office who just talk about it.

Want to grab coffee this week? I can tell you more. Thursday after work?

Em

act2.subject: Great to see you Saturday
act2.body:
Sarah,

Great to see you Saturday. You're really fitting in with the group. People notice that. You have a way of making everyone feel comfortable.

I've been meaning to tell you: some of the people in our circle think you could be really valuable to what we're building. Not just the padel: the broader network. We help each other out, share information, make connections. It's how things actually get done.

No pressure. Just wanted you to know you're appreciated.

Paul

act2.subject: Small favor
act2.body:
Sarah,

Quick favor. We're putting together some research on industry trends. A few of us are working on a side project about corporate procurement patterns. Academic stuff, nothing fancy.

Could you bring the latest quarterly client report when you come Saturday? Just the summary page. I want to cross-reference some numbers for the research.

No big deal if you can't. Just thought since you have easy access it'd save us time.

See you Saturday.

Paul

act2.subject: Is this normal?
act2.body:
Em,

This might sound paranoid but Paul asked me to bring a work document to Saturday's session. A client report. He said it's for some research project.

Is this normal? Did he ever ask you to do anything like that?

I feel weird about it but maybe I'm overthinking.

Sarah

act2.subject: Re: Is this normal?
act2.body:
Yeah, he asked me something similar a while back. A quarterly summary from my department. He said it was for benchmarking or something.

I brought it. It felt odd for a second but then we just played padel and went to dinner and it was totally normal. He never brought it up again after that.

I think it's just how the group works. They're all connected to business stuff. It probably means they trust us enough to include us in the research.

Try not to worry about it. These people have been nothing but good to us.

Em

act2.subject: You're part of the inner circle now
act2.body:
Sarah,

I want you to know how much the group values what you've been contributing. The insights you've shared have been really useful. People are talking about you in a good way.

You're part of the inner circle now. That means something. We look out for each other. We share what we know. And we protect each other.

I know that might sound intense but it's just how a real network operates. You're one of us now and that comes with support you won't find anywhere else.

Proud of you.

Paul

act2.subject: Is it just me or are the asks getting weird?
act2.body:
Sarah,

I need to talk to you about something and I don't want to do it over the phone.

The requests are getting weird. Last week Paul wanted internal correspondence from my department. Not summaries, actual emails. And the way he asked: it wasn't a favor anymore. It was more like an instruction.

He's different with us now than he was at the beginning. Less friendly, more direct. Like he expects things instead of asking for them.

Am I crazy? Tell me I'm imagining this.

Em

act2.subject: I've noticed
act2.body:
You're not imagining it. I've noticed the same thing.

He asked me for the vendor comparison spreadsheet I maintain for one of Daniel's projects last week. Not a summary, the actual internal document. When I hesitated he said something like "you've already done so much, it would be a shame to stop now." Like he was reminding me of everything I've already given them.

It felt like a threat, Em. Not an obvious one but I could feel it.

I don't like the way he talks to me now. Like I owe him something. Like I can't say no.

What did we get ourselves into?

Sarah

act2.subject: We need to talk
act2.body:
Sarah,

We need to talk. In person. Saturday at 2 PM, just us.

I've noticed you've been hesitant lately. I understand, this can feel like a lot. But I want to be honest with you: you've been part of this for months now. You've contributed. You've shared things. That can't be undone.

We value you. We don't want to lose you. And honestly, it wouldn't be good for anyone if you stepped back now. Not for you, not for us.

Saturday. Private meeting room at the club. Come alone.

Paul

act2.subject: I need to get out
act2.body:
Sarah,

I can't do this anymore. I need to get out.

I tried to tell Paul I was done and he wouldn't respond to my emails. Then he showed up at my desk at work, AT WORK, and said we should "talk about my future with the group." He was smiling but his eyes were dead. I've never been so scared sitting in my own office.

They know everything I've done. Every document I brought, every email I forwarded. If I try to leave they'll use it against me. I'll lose my job. Maybe worse.

I don't know what he'll do if I push back. I don't know what these people are capable of.

I made a huge mistake. We both did.

I'm scared, Sarah. I don't know what to do.

Em

act2.subject: Emily please just hold on
act2.body:
Emily, please don't do anything right now. Don't confront Paul again. Don't go to the Saturday sessions. Just stay quiet and let me think.

Can you call me tonight? Not from your work phone. Use this email or call my personal number.

There has to be a way out of this. I won't let them hurt you. Just hold on.

Sarah

act2.subject: Emily's been distracted
act2.body:
Sarah,

Emily has been distracted lately. I think the pressure at work is getting to her. She's been making noise about stepping back from the group, which concerns me.

I need you to talk to her. Remind her what she's part of. Remind her that we're all in this together and that leaving isn't something anyone has done successfully. That's not a threat. It's just reality. People who walk away from networks like ours tend to find that their professional reputation doesn't survive the decision.

She'll listen to you. You're her closest friend in the group.

I'm counting on you.

Paul

act2.subject: Ansible contract intel
act2.body:
Sarah,

I need everything you have on the Ansible Group contract. The strategy deck Daniel is putting together, the timeline Richard sent, and whatever else crosses your desk as this moves. All of it.

This is important. More important than anything I've asked for before. Bring what exists Saturday. Paper copies, not digital.

And keep it coming. Every update, every revision. I want it the day you see it.

Paul

act2.subject: I can't keep doing this
act2.body:
Paul,

No.

I can't bring you more confidential documents. What you're asking me to do could put me in prison. I could lose everything.

I'm done. I won't do it anymore. Whatever happens, happens.

Sarah

act2.subject: Saturday meeting - storage room
act2.body:
Sarah,

I hear you. I do. And I respect that you're being honest about how you feel.

But we need to talk about this face to face. Not over email, not over the phone. In person.

Saturday, 2 PM. The storage room downstairs at Greenside. Not the main courts: go through the side entrance by the parking lot and take the stairs down. It's private and we won't be interrupted.

Just us. We'll figure this out together.

Paul

act2.subject: Thursday at the club
act2.body:
Sarah,

Thursday. 7 PM. Back room at the club. Don't be late.

We need to finalize the Ansible materials. I know you said you were done but that's not how this works. You know that by now.

Don't tell anyone you're coming.

Paul

efb.subject: (No Subject)
efb.body:
Daniel, they gave me my phone for a minute. I'm dictating this. Emily is dead. They killed her. I can't explain right now. Check my private email, you have the password. Please just look at everything. You'll understand. I'm at

What are you doing. Give that back right now. She wasn't supposed to

finalBatch.subject: We found her
finalBatch.body:
Detective,

Your submission checked out. Units entered the Greenside Padel Club forty minutes ago and found Sarah Colvin in a storage room beneath the facility. Dehydrated and shaken, but alive. She's at St. Mary's now, stable, talking.

Paul Martino was taken into custody at his residence without incident. He had her phone on him. We're pulling document archives out of that basement as I write this. Looks like years of material, from more companies than one.

Emily Tan's case has been reclassified as a homicide. Her family has been notified.

Good work, Detective. This one could have ended very differently.

finalBatch.subject: thank you
finalBatch.body:
They told me a detective found me by reading my emails. All of them, I suppose. I don't really know how to feel about that. Mostly grateful. A little exposed.

I'm okay. Emily wasn't so lucky and I can't write about that yet.

I'm going to stay with my sister for a while. Don't call yet. I'll write when I'm ready.

S.

finalBatch.subject: Personnel Announcement
finalBatch.body:
Dear colleagues,

We are writing to inform you that Paul Martino is no longer employed by Veridian Corporation, effective immediately.

As this matter is the subject of an ongoing legal proceeding, we are unable to share further details at this time. We kindly ask that you refrain from speculation.

Employees who wish to talk to someone may contact the Employee Assistance Program through the HR portal. Please direct any media inquiries to Corporate Communications.

Thank you for your understanding.

HR Department
